items.headline: Wall Street Sees Mixed Trading: Dow Reaches Record High, While Nasdaq and S&P 500 Decline Ahead of Fed Decision
items.summary: U.S. equities closed mixed on Tuesday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average extending its record-setting rally, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 pulled back. Investors engaged in sector rotation, shifting out of technology stocks into more economically sensitive sectors. Oil prices experienced a significant decline amid growing optimism regarding a potential U.S.-Iran peace agreement and the easing of supply concerns. All eyes are now on the Federal Reserve's policy announcement later today.

items.statContext: Down 0.6% (or 42.94 points). Futures slipped slightly in early trading.
Down 1.2% (or 307.60 points). Futures edged higher in premarket.
Up 0.6% (or 328.64 points), setting a new record high. Futures saw a slight decline in premarket.

items.headline: SpaceX Rallies on Acquisition News
items.summary: SpaceX rose for its third consecutive session since its U.S. stock market debut, gaining 4.8% after announcing it is moving forward with the acquisition of AI coding assistant Cursor.

items.headline: Honeywell Aerospace Surges on Director Appointment
items.summary: Honeywell Aerospace surged after appointing AT&T CFO Pascal Desroches as an independent director and audit chair.

items.headline: CoreWeave Jumps on Strong Performance Commentary
items.summary: CoreWeave saw a significant jump following commentary from Cantor Fitzgerald highlighting strong backlog and MLPerf benchmark performance.

items.headline: Marvell Technology Leads Chipmaker Pullback
items.summary: Marvell Technology fell as chipmakers broadly pulled back after recent gains, contributing to the broader tech sector decline.

items.headline: Micron Technology Drops Amid Tech Weakness
items.summary: Micron Technology experienced a 6.2% drop, joining other influential tech stocks weighing on the S&P 500.

items.headline: Olin Falls After Acquisition Announcement
items.summary: Chemical producer Olin declined after announcing a $2.43 billion all-stock acquisition of Huntsman.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Poised to Hold Rates Steady Amidst Policy Meeting Conclusion
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is expected to conclude its two-day June meeting today, widely anticipated to keep the federal funds rate unchanged within the 3.50%-3.75% range. Investors are keenly awaiting new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first press conference for insights into future rate trajectory and potential shifts in the central bank's communication strategy.

items.headline: Oil Prices Tumble Below $80 on U.S.-Iran Peace Hopes
items.summary: Crude oil prices plummeted on Tuesday, with Brent crude falling below $80 per barrel for the first time since March. The decline was attributed to growing optimism surrounding a preliminary U.S.-Iran peace agreement, which could ease concerns about global oil supply disruptions, particularly from the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Technology Sector Lagging as Investors Rotate into Cyclical Stocks
items.summary: Wall Street saw a notable sector rotation, with investors selling off richly valued technology shares, making Technology the weakest-performing sector, down 2.3%. Conversely, economically sensitive sectors such as Financials (+1.5%) and Industrials (+0.7%) were among the leading gainers.

items.headline: Key Economic Data on Deck: Retail Sales Today, PCE & GDP Next Week
items.summary: Today's economic calendar includes the release of Advance Retail Sales data. Looking ahead, markets are anticipating the May Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation and the final Q1 2026 GDP estimates, both scheduled for release on June 25. U.S. industrial production in May increased by a modest 0.1%.

items.headline: CarMax, Jabil, and Smith & Wesson Among Companies Reporting Earnings Today
items.summary: Several major companies are scheduled to report their quarterly earnings today, June 17, including CarMax ($KMX), Jabil ($JBL), and Smith & Wesson Brands ($SWBI). Powerfleet ($AIOT) also held its Q4 earnings call, signaling a focus on fiscal year 2027 growth and margin expansion.
